description: A monotonically increasing shard key sends all new writes to one shard.
body: Failure mode: sharding on an increasing field such as a timestamp or default ObjectId routes every new insert to the chunk holding the maximum value, so one shard absorbs all writes while others sit idle. Detection signals: sh.status() shows a single shard owning the top chunk, write latency concentrates on one node, and the balancer churns trying to split a hot chunk. Do not use a monotonic field alone as the shard key. Safer fallback: use a hashed shard key or a compound key whose leading field has high, non-sequential cardinality, and reshard the collection onto the new key during a maintenance window.
